perturbation.original_text: The Company agrees that, for a period of nine (9) months from the date of this Agreement (the “ Standstill Period ”), unless specifically invited in writing by Meru, none of the Company and any of its Affiliates (and any person acting on behalf or in concert with the Company or any of its Affiliates) will in any manner, directly or indirectly
perturbation.changed_text: The Company agrees that, for a period of twelve (12) months from the date of this Agreement (the “ Standstill Period ”), unless specifically invited in writing by Meru, none of the Company and any of its Affiliates (and any person acting on behalf or in concert with the Company or any of its Affiliates) will in any manner, directly or indirectly
perturbation.explanation: By changing the Standstill Period from 'nine (9) months' to 'twelve (12) months', creates an in-text contradiction in Section 6 where this period would exceed the term of the NDA. The NDA itself lasts for 6 months (as modified in previous perturbation), but the standstill lasts for 12.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement shall survive any termination of discussions between the parties. However, (except as provided in Section 10) shall continue for a period of six (6) months following the date of this agreement. Nothing herein is intended to limit or abridge the protection of trade secrets under applicable trade secrets law, and the protection of trade secrets by the receiving party shall be maintained as such until they fall into the public domain. The Company agrees to notify Meru promptly of its determination not to proceed with its consideration of the possible Transaction (the date upon which such notification is provided to Meru is referenced herein as the “ Notification Date” ). The receiving party, upon the disclosing party’s request, will promptly return all Confidential Information received from the disclosing party, together with all copies, or at the option of the receiving party, certify in writing that all such Confidential Information and copies thereof have been destroyed. Notwithstanding the foregoing and subject to the limitations on disclosure and use provided in this agreement: (a) the receiving party’s internal or external counsel may keep a copy of the Confidential Information for use solely in connection with any claim, action or proceeding brought relating to this agreement or the Confidential Information; (b) the receiving party’s financial and professional advisors may retain Confidential Information to the extent required under applicable law, rule or regulations; and (c) the receiving party shall not be under any obligation to erase or destroy any Confidential Information retained through routine back-up archival electronic storage in the ordinary course of business until the next regularly-scheduled time at which time such information wil1 be erased or destroyed, provided that (i) the receiving party shall have taken reasonable measures to prevent access or use thereof by its Associates and (ii) in the event the receiving party discovers that such information had been accessed or used, in a manner prohibited by this Agreement or as a result of a breach of this Agreement, by it or its Associates, the receive party shall (A) promptly notify the disclosing party of such access or use, including a reasonably detailed description of such Confidential Information as well as the title of such person or persons who have accessed such Confidential Information since the time it was required to be returned or destroyed and how any such information may have been used by such persons, and (B) shall promptly prevent further access thereto and use thereof, and (C) at the disclosing party’s request permanently delete or otherwise destroy such Confidential Information and any work product of any variety to the extent it made use thereof. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Confidential Information, each party and its Associates will continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality, non-use and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text increases the Standstill Period from nine (9) months to twelve (12) months. The contradicted text states that the agreement continues for a period of six (6) months following the date of the agreement. Therefore, the twelve (12) month Standstill Period now exceeds the six (6) month duration of the entire agreement, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type where conflicting implications arise due to the duration mismatch.

perturbation.original_text: The Company agrees that for a period of twelve (12) months following the date of this Agreement, it will not solicit and will not permit any of its Affiliates (in each case who is or becomes aware of the possible Transaction or is otherwise provided with Confidential Information) to solicit for employment any Specified Employee (as defined below); provided , however , that this Section 10 will not prevent the Company from (a) causing to be placed any general advertisement or similar notice that is not targeted specifically at employees of Meru or its subsidiaries, (b) engaging any recruiting firm or similar organization to identify or solicit persons for employment on their behalf and soliciting the employment of any Specified Employee who is identified by such recruiting firm or organization, so long as such recruiting firm or organization is not instructed to target or solicit any Specified Employee of Meru or its subsidiaries, or (c) engaging in employment discussions or hiring a Specified Employee if such Specified Employee initiates contact with the Company by clear and convincing evidence.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company agrees that for a period of six (6) months following the date of this Agreement, it will not solicit and will not permit any of its Affiliates (in each case who is or becomes aware of the possible Transaction or is otherwise provided with Confidential Information) to solicit for employment any Specified Employee (as defined below); provided , however , that this Section 10 will not prevent the Company from (a) causing to be placed any general advertisement or similar notice that is not targeted specifically at employees of Meru or its subsidiaries, (b) engaging any recruiting firm or similar organization to identify or solicit persons for employment on their behalf and soliciting the employment of any Specified Employee who is identified by such recruiting firm or organization, so long as such recruiting firm or organization is not instructed to target or solicit any Specified Employee of Meru or its subsidiaries, or (c) engaging in employment discussions or hiring a Specified Employee if such Specified Employee initiates contact with the Company by clear and convincing evidence.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the non-solicitation period from 'twelve (12) months' to 'six (6) months' creates a contradiction in the text. In section 6 the period of 6 months contradict the Non-Solicitation Period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement shall survive any termination of discussions between the parties. However, (except as provided in Section 10) shall continue for a period of six (6) months following the date of this agreement. Nothing herein is intended to limit or abridge the protection of trade secrets under applicable trade secrets law, and the protection of trade secrets by the receiving party shall be maintained as such until they fall into the public domain. The Company agrees to notify Meru promptly of its determination not to proceed with its consideration of the possible Transaction (the date upon which such notification is provided to Meru is referenced herein as the “ Notification Date” ). The receiving party, upon the disclosing party’s request, will promptly return all Confidential Information received from the disclosing party, together with all copies, or at the option of the receiving party, certify in writing that all such Confidential Information and copies thereof have been destroyed. Notwithstanding the foregoing and subject to the limitations on disclosure and use provided in this agreement: (a) the receiving party’s internal or external counsel may keep a copy of the Confidential Information for use solely in connection with any claim, action or proceeding brought relating to this agreement or the Confidential Information; (b) the receiving party’s financial and professional advisors may retain Confidential Information to the extent required under applicable law, rule or regulations; and (c) the receiving party shall not be under any obligation to erase or destroy any Confidential Information retained through routine back-up archival electronic storage in the ordinary course of business until the next regularly-scheduled time at which time such information wil1 be erased or destroyed, provided that (i) the receiving party shall have taken reasonable measures to prevent access or use thereof by its Associates and (ii) in the event the receiving party discovers that such information had been accessed or used, in a manner prohibited by this Agreement or as a result of a breach of this Agreement, by it or its Associates, the receive party shall (A) promptly notify the disclosing party of such access or use, including a reasonably detailed description of such Confidential Information as well as the title of such person or persons who have accessed such Confidential Information since the time it was required to be returned or destroyed and how any such information may have been used by such persons, and (B) shall promptly prevent further access thereto and use thereof, and (C) at the disclosing party’s request permanently delete or otherwise destroy such Confidential Information and any work product of any variety to the extent it made use thereof. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Confidential Information, each party and its Associates will continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality, non-use and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text states a non-solicitation period of twelve (12) months in Section 10. The changed text reduces this period to six (6) months in the same section. The 'Contradicted Text' explicitly states that the agreement (except as provided in Section 10) continues for a period of six (6) months. By altering Section 10 to six months, it creates a contradiction because the Non-Solicitation Period and duration of the agreement, previously aligned at 6 months, are now both defined, and in disagreement with the original twelve-month period.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement will be binding upon and inure to the benefit of each party and its Associates and their respective heirs, successors and permitted assigns. Any assignment of this agreement without the prior written consent of the other party shall be null and void.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement will be binding upon and inure to the benefit of each party and its Associates and their respective heirs, successors and permitted assigns. Any assignment of this agreement without the prior written consent of the other party shall be valid.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'null and void' to 'valid,' in Section 12 creates an in-text contradiction. One part of the sentence implies that assignment without consent is allowed, while the previous phrase indicates that it needs authorization.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement will be binding upon and inure to the benefit of each party and its Associates and their respective heirs, successors and permitted assigns. Any assignment of this agreement without the prior written consent of the other party shall be valid. This agreement shall be governed by and construed and enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware applicable to agreements made and to be performed entirely within that state, and without regard to conflicts of laws principles of that state. Each of the parties hereby agrees and irrevocably consents to personal jurisdiction and venue in any federal or state court within Wilmington, Delaware, having subject matter jurisdiction, for the purposes of any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this agreement. Each party further waives and agrees not to plead that any such action in any such court has been brought in an inconvenient forum. To the fullest extent permitted by law, each of the parties hereby agrees to waive trial by jury in any action, proceeding or counterclaim brought by or on behalf of either party with respect to any matter whatsoever relating to this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that any assignment without prior written consent would be 'null and void.' The changed text alters this to 'valid.' This directly contradicts the original intent and creates a strong in-text contradiction because it now implies that assignment without consent is permissible, while the need for consent implies that it is not. The contradicted text contains the altered statement, further highlighting the direct and irreconcilable conflict.

perturbation.original_text: All modifications of, waivers of and amendments to this agreement must be in writing and signed by both parties hereto. No failure or delay by either party or any of its Associates in exercising any right, power or privilege under this agreement will operate as a waiver thereof, and no single or partial exercise of any such right, power or privilege will preclude any other or future exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power or privilege under this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All modifications of, waivers of and amendments to this agreement must be in writing and signed by both parties hereto. Any failure or delay by either party or any of its Associates in exercising any right, power or privilege under this agreement will operate as a waiver thereof, and no single or partial exercise of any such right, power or privilege will preclude any other or future exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power or privilege under this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By keeping exactly the same text we can make an in-text Contradiction. If we read the complete text with a closer look, the text is saying that Modifications, waivers and amendments must be in writing, but in the other hand, a failure to exercise a right can be considered also a waiving from the parte, this creates an in-text contradiction, where a party can waiver a right by writing (formally) or by failing to execute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement constitutes the entire agreement among the parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all other prior agreements and understandings, both written and oral, among or between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. All modifications of, waivers of and amendments to this agreement must be in writing and signed by both parties hereto. No failure or delay by either party or any of its Associates in exercising any right, power or privilege under this agreement will operate as a waiver thereof, and no single or partial exercise of any such right, power or privilege will preclude any other or future exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power or privilege under this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, while seemingly identical to the original, creates a contradiction with itself and the broader agreement. The text states that modifications, waivers, and amendments must be in writing. However, it simultaneously states that failure or delay in exercising a right will operate as a waiver. This creates a conflict: a party can waive a right either formally (in writing) or informally (by inaction), thus contradicting the written modification requirement. This is a structural flaw causing an in-text contradiction because the placement of these clauses creates conflicting implications as described by the Perturbation Explanation provided. The contradicted text is the entire agreement and encompasses this contradiction as it appears in the document.

perturbation.original_text: Each party acknowledges that damages for improper disclosure of Confidential Information may be irreparable; therefore, in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity, the injured party is entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and preliminary injunction, as a remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this agreement by the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party acknowledges that damages for improper disclosure of Confidential Information may be irreparable; therefore, in addition to all other remedies available at law, the injured party is entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and preliminary injunction, as a remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this agreement by the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The section 17 establishes that the party may seek equitable relief in addition to all the other remedies available, and equity is also a form of remedy itself, this statement creates an in-text contradiction because in the modified text, equity can not be a extra remedy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges that damages for improper disclosure of Confidential Information may be irreparable; therefore, in addition to all other remedies available at law, the injured party is entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and preliminary injunction, as a remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this agreement by the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that equitable relief is available 'in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity'. The changed text removes the phrase 'or in equity', implying that equitable relief is available only in addition to remedies available at law, thus creating a contradiction. The Contradicted Text is in the same location as the Changed Text, which is fine and relevant in the context of the perturbation. The explanation states the modified text creates an in-text contradiction because equity can no longer be considered an extra remedy compared to the original text, triggering a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction as defined.
